requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative
Banks - Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall become effective from the date of issue.
requirements.object: date of issue

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Urban Co-operative Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'UCBs' and individually as a 'UCB').
requirements.object: Co-operative Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'UCBs' and individually as a 'UCB')

requirements.full_sentence:
In this context, 'Urban Co-operative Banks' shall mean Primary Co-operative
Banks as defined under section 5(ccv) read with Section 56 of the Banking
Regulation Act, 1949.
requirements.object: as defined under section 5(ccv) read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949

requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, unless the context states otherwise, the terms herein shall
bear the meaning assigned to them below:
(1) 'Defeasance periods' is the time taken to liquidate the position on the basis
of liquidity in the secondary market.
requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'Defeasance periods' is the time taken to liquidate the position on the basis of liquidity in the seconda

requirements.full_sentence: (3) 'Level I UCB', for the specific purpose of these Directions, shall mean:

All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, the RBI Act, rules
/ regulations made thereunder, or any statutory modification or re-enactment
thereto or as used in commercial parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, the RBI Act, rules / regulations made thereunder, or any statuto

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall be responsible for overall management of risks, as also for the
formulation of risk management policy and procedures, setting prudential limits,
and establishing mechanisms for audit, reporting, and review in respect of forex
risk (not applicable to a Level I UCB), liquidity risk, and interest rate risk (IRR), .
requirements.object: management of risks, as also for the formulation of risk management policy and procedures, setting prudential limits, and e

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall also oversee the implementation of the ALM system, and
periodically review its functioning.
requirements.object: of the ALM system, and periodically review its functioning

conditions:
if BoM has been constituted by a UCB as per Reserve Bank of India
(Urban Co-operative Banks - Governance) Directions
requirements.full_sentence:
The Board may delegate the above responsibilities to the Board of Management
(BoM), if BoM has been constituted by a UCB as per Reserve Bank of India
(Urban Co-operative Banks - Governance) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: to the Board of Management (BoM)

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve the internal prudential limits for cumulative mismatches
(running total) across all time buckets of Structural Liquidity Statement (SLS) for
monitoring by a UCB.
requirements.object: limits for cumulative mismatches (running total) across all time buckets of Structural Liquidity Statement (SLS) for m

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve the volume, composition, holding / defeasance period,
cut loss, etc., in respect of the Trading Book of a UCB.
requirements.object: holding / defeasance period, cut loss, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall ensure that various statements as provided in Chapter VII are
placed by the UCB before it.
requirements.object: as provided in Chapter VII are placed by the UCB before it

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve prudential limits on individual gaps of Interest Rate
Sensitivity (IRS) statement, which shall be based on Total Assets, Earning
Assets or Equity (not applicable to a Level 1 UCB).
requirements.object: individual gaps of Interest Rate Sensitivity (IRS) statement, which shall be based on Total Assets, Earning Assets or Equ

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall ensure that the IRS statement, along with the analysis of Gaps
and Earnings at Risk (EaR), are placed before the Board, and Board / the Top
Management shall formulate corrective measures and devise suitable strategies
wherever needed, as per information on the IRR provided in the statement (not
applicable to a Level 1 UCB).
requirements.object: statement, along with the analysis of Gaps and Earnings at Risk (EaR), are placed before the Board, and Board / the Top Management

requirements.full_sentence:
Guidelines in this Chapter shall not apply to a Level 1 UCB unless stated
otherwise.
requirements.object: 1 UCB unless stated otherwise

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall introduce an effective Asset Liability Management (ALM) system to
address liquidity risk, IRR, and currency risk.
requirements.object: Liability Management (ALM) system to address liquidity risk, IRR, and currency risk

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall serve as a benchmark.

A UCB that has already
implemented more sophisticated systems may continue using them.

However,
the UCB shall fine-tune its existing system to ensure compliance with the
requirements set out in these Directions.
requirements.object: to ensure compliance with the requirements set out in these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall also review its current
Management Information System (MIS) and implement necessary
enhancements as prescribed.
requirements.object: Management Information System (MIS) and implement necessary enhancements as prescribed

requirements.full_sentence:
Once the ALM system is stabilised and the UCB
has gained sufficient experience, it shall prepare to transition to more
sophisticated techniques for IRR management such as Duration Gap Analysis,
Simulation, and Value at Risk.
requirements.object: more sophisticated techniques for IRR management such as Duration Gap Analysis, Simulation, and Value at Risk

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall implement its ALM process based on the following three pillars:
(1) ALM Information Systems, comprising:
requirements.object: based on the following three pillars: (1) ALM Information Systems, comprising:

requirements.full_sentence:
Top Management of the UCB shall put in place a robust
information system to ensure availability of timely, adequate, and accurate
information.
requirements.object: robust information system to ensure availability of timely, adequate, and accurate information

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall constitute an Asset Liability Management Committee (ALCO), headed
by the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) or the Secretary and consisting of the Top
Management.
requirements.object: Management Committee (ALCO), headed by the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) or the Secretary and consisting of the Top Mana

requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO shall be responsible for ensuring adherence to the
policies and limits approved by the Board and for deciding the business strategy,
on the assets and liabilities sides, in alignment with the UCB's business, and risk
management objectives.
requirements.object: adherence to the policies and limits approved by the Board and for deciding the business strategy, on the assets and liabi

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall oversee the implementation of the
system and periodically review its functioning.
requirements.object: the system and periodically review its functioning

requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO shall act as a decision-making unit responsible for balance sheet
planning from risk-return perspective, including the strategic management of
liquidity, interest rate, and forex risks.
requirements.object: unit responsible for balance sheet planning from risk-return perspective, including the strategic management of liquidity, in

requirements.full_sentence:
The future business strategy decisions of
ALCO shall be based on the UCB's views on current interest rates.
requirements.object: UCB's views on current interest rates

requirements.full_sentence:
As part of
funding strategy, ALCO shall determine the source and mix of liabilities or sale of
assets.
requirements.object: mix of liabilities or sale of assets

requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO shall develop a view on future direction of interest rate
movements and decide on funding mix between fixed v/s.
requirements.object: future direction of interest rate movements and decide on funding mix between fixed v/s

requirements.full_sentence:
The business and risk management strategy of a UCB shall ensure that all
operations remain within the limits and parameters set by the Board.
requirements.object: remain within the limits and parameters set by the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO
shall, inter-alia, consider key business issues such as pricing of deposits and
advances, desired maturity profile, and mix of the incremental assets and liabilities.
requirements.object: business issues such as pricing of deposits and advances, desired maturity profile, and mix of the incremental assets and l

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to monitoring the UCB's risk levels, the ALCO shall review the outcome
of previous decisions and evaluate the progress made in their implementation.
requirements.object: previous decisions and evaluate the progress made in their implementation

requirements.full_sentence:
The composition and size (number of members) of the ALCO shall depend on the
size, level of business, and organisational structure of the UCB.
requirements.object: level of business, and organisational structure of the UCB

requirements.full_sentence:
The CEO or the
Secretary shall head the ALCO, and the Heads of Investment, Credit and Strategy,
Treasury, Risk Management, etc., can be its members, along with other members
as deemed suitable.
requirements.object: the Heads of Investment, Credit and Strategy, Treasury, Risk Management, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
The Head of the Information Technology Division shall be an
invitee.
A UCB, at its discretion, may have Sub-committees and Support Groups.
requirements.object: Groups

requirements.full_sentence: A UCB shall decide the frequency for holding meetings of the ALCO.
requirements.object: holding meetings of the ALCO

requirements.full_sentence:
The ALM Support Groups, consisting of operating staff, shall be responsible for
analysing, monitoring, and reporting the risk profiles to the ALCO.
requirements.object: monitoring, and reporting the risk profiles to the ALCO

requirements.full_sentence:
The Group shall
also prepare forecasts (simulations) showing the effects of various possible
changes in market conditions related to the balance sheet and recommend the
action needed to adhere to UCB's internal limits.
requirements.object: showing the effects of various possible changes in market conditions related to the balance sheet and recommend t

requirements.full_sentence:
The scope of ALM function shall include the following:
(1) Liquidity risk management.
requirements.object: Liquidity risk management

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall measure liquidity positions on an ongoing basis as also examine how
liquidity requirements are likely to evolve under different assumptions / scenarios.
requirements.object: an ongoing basis as also examine how liquidity requirements are likely to evolve under different assumptions / scenario
Stock approach: Analysing liquidity ratios such as credit deposit ratio, loans to total assets, loans to core deposits, and

requirements.full_sentence:
A Scheduled UCB and a non-Scheduled UCB shall prepare the SLS under static
scenario without reckoning the future business growth, as per the reporting format
provided in Annex-I and Annex-II of these Directions, respectively.
requirements.object: static scenario without reckoning the future business growth, as per the reporting format provided in Annex-I and Annex-II of th

requirements.full_sentence: A UCB shall use the maturity profile, given in Annex-VI (for a Scheduled UCB) and
requirements.object: given in Annex-VI (for a Scheduled UCB) and

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall classify all cash inflows and outflows in the maturity ladder according
to the expected timing of cash flows.
requirements.object: and outflows in the maturity ladder according to the expected timing of cash flows

requirements.full_sentence:
A maturing liability shall be a cash outflow
while a maturing asset shall be a cash inflow.
requirements.object: while a maturing asset shall be a cash inflow

requirements.full_sentence:
While determining the probable cash
<image>
inflows / outflows, a UCB shall make assumptions according to its asset liability
profile.
requirements.object: its asset liability profile

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall monitor its cumulative mismatches (running total) across all time
buckets by establishing internal prudential limits with the approval of the Board.
requirements.object: (running total) across all time buckets by establishing internal prudential limits with the approval of the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
Tolerance levels for mismatches shall be fixed, taking into account the asset-
liability profile, nature of business, and future strategy.
requirements.object: account the asset- liability profile, nature of business, and future strategy

requirements.full_sentence:
While the mismatches upto
one year would be relevant since these provide early warning signals of impending
liquidity problems, the main focus should be on short term mismatches.
requirements.object: mismatches

requirements.full_sentence:
For a non-Scheduled UCB (including Level I UCB), the negative mismatches
(negative gap) in the 1-14 days, and 15-28 days time buckets shall, under normal
circumstances, not exceed 20 per cent of the cash outflows in the respective time
bucket.
requirements.object: not exceed 20 per cent of the cash outflows in the respective time bucket

requirements.full_sentence:
A Scheduled UCB shall adopt a more granular approach to measurement of
liquidity risk by splitting the first time bucket (1-14 days) in the SLS into three time
buckets, viz., next day, 2-7 days, and 8-14 days.
requirements.object: approach to measurement of liquidity risk by splitting the first time bucket (1-14 days) in the SLS into three time buckets, viz

requirements.full_sentence:
A Scheduled UCB shall ensure
that the net cumulative negative mismatches during the next day, 2-7 days, 8-14
days, and 15-28 days buckets shall not exceed 5 per cent, 10 per cent, 15 per cent
and 20 per cent of the cumulative cash outflows in the respective time buckets.
requirements.object: 10 per cent, 15 per cent and 20 per cent of the cumulative cash outflows in the respective time buckets

requirements.full_sentence:
Investments in SLR securities and other investments shall be placed under the
residual maturity buckets, corresponding to their remaining maturities.
requirements.object: residual maturity buckets, corresponding to their remaining maturities

conditions: in case of a Scheduled UCB)
requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB maintaining securities in the Trading Book, kept distinct from other
investments made for complying with the Statutory Reserve requirements and for
retaining customer relationship, shall classify such securities under 1-14 days (split
into next day, 2-7 days, and 8-14 days in case of a Scheduled UCB), 15-28 days,
and 29-90 days' time buckets, based on their defeasance periods, provided the
trading book meets the following criteria:
(1) Clearly defined composition and volume;
requirements.object: 1-14 days (split into next day, 2-7 days, and 8-14 days in case of a Scheduled UCB), 15-28 days, and 29-90 days' time buckets, based on their defeasance periods

conditions: in case of a Level I UCB) shall approve the volume
requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO of the UCB (Board in case of a Level I UCB) shall approve the volume,
composition, holding / defeasance period, cut loss, etc., of the Trading Book.
requirements.object: holding / defeasance period, cut loss, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB with Authorised Dealer (AD) licence shall take into account the ₹ inflows
and outflows on account of its forex operations while preparing the prescribed
liquidity statements.
requirements.object: ₹ inflows and outflows on account of its forex operations while preparing the prescribed liquidity statements

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding guidance provided in Annex-VI and Annex-VII of these Directions,
a UCB which is better equipped to reasonably estimate the behavioural pattern of
various components of assets and liabilities on the basis of past data / empirical
studies shall classify them in the appropriate time buckets, subject to approval from
the ALCO.
requirements.object: appropriate time buckets

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall estimate its short-term liquidity profile on the basis of business
projections and other commitments for planning purposes, as per indicative format
for Short-term Dynamic Liquidity statement in Annex-III.
requirements.object: profile on the basis of business projections and other commitments for planning purposes, as per indicative format f

requirements.full_sentence:
The list of such Scheduled UCBs which meet the eligibility norms to participate in
LAF and MSF (Positive List), and of those Scheduled UCBs found ineligible
(Negative List), shall be prepared by the Reserve Bank of India.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB with AD license shall set Gap limits and adopt Value at Risk (VaR) approach
to measure the risk associated with forex exposures.
requirements.object: adopt Value at Risk (VaR) approach to measure the risk associated with forex exposures

requirements.full_sentence:
The open position limits
together with the Gap limits shall form part of the risk management approach for
the UCB's forex operations.
requirements.object: risk management approach for the UCB's forex operations

requirements.full_sentence:
The provision of this Chapter shall not apply to a Level I UCB, unless stated
otherwise.
requirements.object: I UCB

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall implement appropriate systems to measure and manage IRR, arising
on account of deregulation of interest rates, and operational flexibility in pricing of
assets and liabilities.
requirements.object: measure and manage IRR, arising on account of deregulation of interest rates, and operational flexibility in pricing

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall employ suitable analytical tools for the measurement and
management of IRR.
requirements.object: for the measurement and management of IRR

requirements.full_sentence:
The UCB shall initially use the Traditional Gap Analysis (TGA)
for measuring IRR.
requirements.object: Gap Analysis (TGA) for measuring IRR

requirements.full_sentence:
Under TGA, a UCB shall measure Gap or Mismatch risk by calculating Gaps, i.e.,
the difference between Rate Sensitive Assets (RSA) and Rate Sensitive Liabilities
(RSL) over different time intervals as on a given date.
requirements.object: risk by calculating Gaps, i

requirements.full_sentence:
A Gap Report shall be generated by grouping
rate sensitive liabilities, assets, and off-balance sheet positions into time buckets
according to residual maturity or next repricing period, whichever is earlier.
requirements.object: rate sensitive liabilities, assets, and off-balance sheet positions into time buckets according to residual maturity or next

requirements.full_sentence:
All investments, advances, deposits, borrowings, and other instruments that
mature / have a cash flow or reprice within a specific time frame shall be treated
as interest rate sensitive.
requirements.object: rate sensitive

requirements.full_sentence:
Repayments of loan instalments, including both principal and periodic payments,
shall also be treated as rate sensitive if they are expected to be received within the
time horizon.
requirements.object: rate sensitive if they are expected to be received within the time horizon

requirements.full_sentence:
Assets and liabilities that are linked to a reference rate that reprices at pre-
determined intervals, shall be treated as rate sensitive at the time of contractual
repricing.
requirements.object: sensitive at the time of contractual repricing

requirements.full_sentence: A UCB shall prepare the IRS statement, as per the reporting format provided in
requirements.object: as per the reporting format provided in

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall report only ₹ assets, liabilities, and off-balance sheet positions in the
IRS statement.
requirements.object: liabilities, and off-balance sheet positions in the IRS statement

requirements.full_sentence:
The rate sensitive assets, liabilities, and off-balance sheet positions shall be
grouped into the following time buckets according to residual maturity or next
repricing date, whichever is earlier:
(1) Upto 3 months
(2) Over 3 months and upto 6 months
(3) Over 6 months and upto 1 year
(4) Over 1 year and upto 3 years
(5) Over 3 years and upto 5 years
(6) Over 5 years
(7) Non-sensitive
58.
requirements.object: following time buckets according to residual maturity or next repricing date, whichever is earlier: (1) Upto 3 months (2) Over 3 m

requirements.full_sentence:
The various items of rate sensitive assets and liabilities, and off-balance sheet
items shall be classified into time buckets as per guidance given in Annex-VIII
(Scheduled UCB) and Annex-IX (non-Scheduled UCB).
requirements.object: buckets as per guidance given in Annex-VIII (Scheduled UCB) and Annex-IX (non-Scheduled UCB)

requirements.full_sentence:
Gap Reports shall be used by the UCB to assess the extent of IRR
and to formulate IRR mitigation strategies.
requirements.object: UCB to assess the extent of IRR and to formulate IRR mitigation strategies

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall set prudential limits on individual Gaps with the approval of its Board,
which shall be based on Total Assets, Earning Assets or Equity levels of the UCB.
requirements.object: individual Gaps with the approval of its Board, which shall be based on Total Assets, Earning Assets or Equity levels of the

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
A UCB shall also work out Earnings at Risk (EaR) in terms of last year's NII, or Net
Interest Margin (NIM) aligned with its views on interest rate movements.
requirements.object: at Risk (EaR) in terms of last year's NII, or Net Interest Margin (NIM) aligned with its views on interest rate movements

requirements.full_sentence:
The IRS statement, along with the analysis of Gaps and EaR, shall be placed
before the Board in its next meeting.
requirements.object: Board in its next meeting

requirements.full_sentence:
The Top Management / Board shall formulate
corrective measures and devise suitable strategies wherever needed, as per
information on IRR provided in the statement.
requirements.object: devise suitable strategies wherever needed, as per information on IRR provided in the statement

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the guidance provided in Annex-VIII and Annex-IX of these
Directions, a UCB which is better equipped to reasonably estimate the behavioural
pattern of various components of assets and liabilities on the basis of past data /
empirical studies shall classify them in the appropriate time buckets based on the
behavioural patterns, subject to approval from the ALCO.
requirements.object: appropriate time buckets based on the behavioural patterns

requirements.full_sentence:
A UCB shall refer to the Reserve Bank of India (Filing of Supervisory Returns)
Directions - 2024 dated February 27, 2024, for instructions with respect to
periodicity and timelines of returns consolidated as under:
B.1 Scheduled UCB:
Return Periodicity
Structural Liquidity Statement (ALM3) Fortnightly
Short-term Dynamic Liquidity statement (ALM2) Fortnightly
Interest Rate Sensitivity Statement (ALM1) Monthly
B.2 Non-Scheduled UCB (excluding UCB in Level I):
Return Periodicity
Structural Liquidity Statement (ALM3) Quarterly
Interest Rate Sensitivity Statement (ALM1) Quarterly
B.3 UCB in Level I:
Return Periodicity
Structural Liquidity Statement (ALM3) Quarterly
64.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions - 2024 dated February 27, 2024, for instructions with respect to periodi

requirements.full_sentence: A UCB shall submit the ALM returns correctly and within the prescribed time.
requirements.object: correctly and within the prescribed time

requirements.full_sentence:
It
shall designate and authorise one or two senior official/s who shall be responsible
for the correct compilation and timely submission of these returns and who shall
be fully responsible for the information furnished therein.
requirements.object: or two senior official/s who shall be responsible for the correct compilation and timely submission of these returns and w

requirements.full_sentence:
Such designated
Authorised Reporting Official/s (ARO/s) shall liaison with the officials in the
Department of Supervision (DoS), RBI.
requirements.object: in the Department of Supervision (DoS), RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
The names and designations of the ARO/s
shall be indicated to the concerned Regional Office of DoS, RBI while forwarding
these returns.
requirements.object: concerned Regional Office of DoS, RBI while forwarding these returns

requirements.full_sentence:
The
Directions, instructions and guidelines already repealed shall continue to remain
repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions
affect (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those Directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those Directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being
in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
<image>
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of
these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
Demand Deposits (Current and Savings Bank Deposits) Savings Bank and Current Deposits may be classified into volatile and core portions.
requirements.object: and core portions

requirements.full_sentence: This portion may be treated as volatile.

While volatile portion can be placed in the Day 1, 2-7 days and 8-14 days time buckets, depending upon the experience and estimates of banks and the core portion may be placed in over 1- 3 years bucket.
requirements.object: 1- 3 years bucket

requirements.full_sentence: However, the wholesale deposits should be shown under respective maturity buckets.
requirements.object: maturity buckets

requirements.full_sentence: (wholesale deposits for the purpose of this statement may be ₹ 15 lakh or any such higher threshold approved by the UCB's Board).
requirements.object: or any such higher threshold approved by the UCB's Board)

conditions: Where call / put options are built into the issue structure of any instrument/s
requirements.full_sentence: Where call / put options are built into the issue structure of any instrument/s, the call / put date/s should be reckoned as the maturity date/s and the amount should be shown in the respective time buckets.
requirements.object: maturity date/s and the amount should be shown in the respective time buckets

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Bills Payable (i) The core component which could reasonably be estimated on the basis of past data and behavioural pattern may be shown under
<image>
Heads of Accounts Classification into time buckets
'Over 1-3 years' time bucket.
requirements.object: Heads of Accounts Classification into time buckets 'Over 1-3 years' time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: The balance amount may be placed in Day 1, 2- 7 days and 8-14 days buckets, as per behavioural pattern.
requirements.object: 1, 2- 7 days and 8-14 days buckets, as per behavioural pattern

requirements.full_sentence: income received in advance, etc.) may be placed in over 5 years bucket.
requirements.object: 5 years bucket

requirements.full_sentence:
Balances with RBI / Public sector banks and SCBs and DCCBs for CRR / SLR purpose
While the excess balance over the required CRR / SLR may be shown under Day 1 bucket, the Statutory Balances may be distributed amongst various time buckets corresponding to the maturity profile of DTL with a time-lag of 14 days.
requirements.object: 1 bucket, the Statutory Balances may be distributed amongst various time buckets corresponding to the maturity profile of DTL with

requirements.full_sentence: (i) Current Account (i) Non-withdrawable portion on account of stipulations of minimum balances may be shown under 'Over 1-3 years' bucket and the remaining balances may be shown under Day 1 bucket.
requirements.object: 1-3 years' bucket and the remaining balances may be shown under Day 1 bucket

requirements.full_sentence: Investments classified as NPIs should be shown under over 3-5 years bucket (sub-standard) or over 5 years bucket (doubtful).
requirements.object: 3-5 years bucket (sub-standard) or over 5 years bucket (doubtful)

requirements.full_sentence: # Provisions may be netted from the gross investments provided provisions are held security-wise.
requirements.object: gross investments provided provisions are held security-wise

requirements.full_sentence: Other-wise provisions should be shown in over 5 years bucket.
requirements.object: 5 years bucket

requirements.full_sentence: (ii) Banks should undertake a study of behavioural and seasonal pattern of availments based on outstandings and the core and volatile portion should be identified.
requirements.object: behavioural and seasonal pattern of availments based on outstandings and the core and volatile portion should be identified

requirements.full_sentence: While the volatile portion could be shown in the near-term maturity buckets, the core portion may be shown under 'Over 1-3 years' bucket.
requirements.object: 1-3 years' bucket

requirements.full_sentence: (iii) Term Loans (iii) Interim cash flows may be shown under respective maturity buckets.
requirements.object: maturity buckets

requirements.full_sentence: Fixed Assets / Assets on lease 'Over 5 years' bucket / Interim cash flows may be shown under respective maturity buckets.
requirements.object: maturity buckets

requirements.full_sentence: (i) Intangible assets Intangible assets and assets not representing cash receivables may be shown in 'Over 5 years' bucket.
requirements.object: 5 years' bucket

requirements.full_sentence: (ii) Banks should undertake a study of the behavioural and seasonal pattern of potential availments in the accounts and the amounts so arrived at may be shown under relevant maturity buckets upto 12 months.
requirements.object: maturity buckets upto 12 months

requirements.full_sentence:
Thus, historical trend analysis ought to be conducted on the
<image>
Heads of Accounts Classification into time buckets devolvements and the amounts so arrived at in respect of outstanding Letters of Credit / Guarantees (net of margins) should be distributed amongst various time buckets.
requirements.object: time buckets

requirements.full_sentence: The assets created out of devolvements may be shown under respective maturity buckets on the basis of probable recovery dates.
requirements.object: maturity buckets on the basis of probable recovery dates

requirements.full_sentence:
Demand Deposits (Current and Savings Bank Deposits
Savings Bank and Current Deposits may be classified into volatile and core portions.
requirements.object: and core portions

requirements.full_sentence: This portion may therefore be treated as volatile.
requirements.object: volatile

requirements.full_sentence: While volatile portion can be placed in the first time bucket, i.e., 1- 14 days, the core portion may be placed in over 1-3 years time bucket.
requirements.object: 1-3 years time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: However, the wholesale deposits (deposits over Rs.15 lakhs and interbank deposits) should be shown under respective residual time buckets.
requirements.object: residual time buckets

requirements.full_sentence: Branch Adjustments The net credit balance may be shown in 1-14 days time bucket.
requirements.object: days time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: Items not representing cash payables (i.e., guarantee fee received in advance, etc.) may be placed in over 5 years time buckets.
requirements.object: 5 years time buckets

requirements.full_sentence: While the excess balance over the required CRR / SLR may be shown under 1-14 days time buckets, the Statutory Balances may be distributed amongst various time buckets corresponding to the maturity profile of DTL with a time- lag of 28 days.

requirements.full_sentence:
Balances with other Banks

(i) Current Account (i) Non-withdrawable portion on account of stipulations of minimum balances may be shown under over 1-3 years time bucket and the remaining balances may be shown under 1-14 days time bucket.
requirements.object: 1-3 years time bucket and the remaining balances may be shown under 1-14 days time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: Investments classified as NPIs should be shown under over 3-5 years time buckets (sub-standard) or over 5 years time bucket (doubtful).
requirements.object: 3-5 years time buckets (sub-standard) or over 5 years time bucket (doubtful)

requirements.full_sentence: Units of eligible categories of mutual funds (open ended) may be kept under 1-14 days bucket.
requirements.object: days bucket

requirements.full_sentence: While the volatile portion could be shown in the near-term maturity time buckets, the core portion may be shown under over 1-3 year time bucket.
requirements.object: 1-3 year time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: iii) Term Loans (iii) Interim cash flows (instalments) should be shown under respective maturity time buckets.
requirements.object: maturity time buckets

requirements.full_sentence:
Other Assets

i) Branch Adjustments The net debit balance may be shown in 1-14 days time bucket.
requirements.object: days time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: Intangible assets and assets not representing cash receivables may be shown in over 5 years time bucket.
requirements.object: 5 years time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: ii) Leased Assets Interim cash flows may be shown under respective residual maturity time buckets.
requirements.object: residual maturity time buckets

requirements.full_sentence: (i) Banks should undertake a study of the behavioural and seasonal pattern of potential availments in the accounts and the amounts so arrived at may be shown under relevant residual maturity-time buckets within 12 months.

Letters of Credit / Guarantees devolvement (outflow)
Based on past history, these should be distributed across time buckets.
